name: Black Bean Nachos
recipeIngredient: Stems from 2 bunches of Cilantro (fresh coriander)
recipeInstructions:
For Black Bean Pesto:
1. Rinse and drain the dried black beans thoroughly.
2. Place the beans in a large saucepan or soup kettle and add water, bay leaf, ham hock, jalapenos, garlic, cilantro stems, salt, and pepper.
3. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 1 1/2 hours.
4. Remove the ham hock and bay leaf using a slotted spoon. Transfer the bean mixture to a food processor in batches and process until smooth and thick. Use about 1 cup of liquid from the beans.
5. Stir in salt and pepper if needed, then transfer to a bowl. Refrigerate, covered, until ready to use. The pesto will keep in the fridge for 2-3 days.
Nachos:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Fill a heavy skillet with about 1/2 inch of corn oil and heat until it starts to move.
3. Fry the tortillas one at a time, turning them after about 15 seconds on each side, then drain on paper towels.
4. Slice chorizo into 1/4-inch rounds and sauté in the skillet until crisp, about 5 minutes. Remove sausage and drain on paper towel.
5. Place the tortillas on a baking sheet, spread pesto evenly over them, arrange sausage over the pesto, sprinkle with cheese, and top with chopped cilantro.
6. Bake for 20 minutes or until browned. Remove from the oven and cut each tortilla into eight pieces. Serve immediately.
Makes 4 servings or 16 appetizers
